Flag: Encoder outputs swing to Vcc (3.5-20V); 3.3V below floor. Feeding 5V enc outputs to Pi GPIO can damage the pin.
Recommended Action: DONE pending wiring: BSS138 4-ch level converter received. Enc Vcc 5V -> BSS138 -> 3.3V Pi.

Flag: Stance/tip-over: 80mm wheel > 72mm body; caster+shim height vs axle for level deck; big pack mass CG. IMU now helps detect tip.
Recommended Action: Set caster shim to wheel axle height; mount pack low/centered; use IMU tilt in reflex layer.

Flag: Fuse stops a short, NOT LiPo over-discharge. No runtime low-voltage cutoff in inventory yet.
Recommended Action: ADS1115 ADC received - read pack V via divider for runtime low-voltage cutoff.

Flag: KCD1 rocker needs ~19x13mm panel cutout; not in locked shell. Visible black switch also tension w/ no-anthropomorphism spine.
Recommended Action: Decide placement (underside/rear vs intentional cue) BEFORE top shell is printed.

Flag: B6AC V2 needs a charge lead that mates to pack XT60; may not be in charger bundle.
Recommended Action: Confirm bundle; if absent, build XT60 charge lead from spare XT60H.

Flag: Body-to-ridge bus: locked as 6-wire JST-XH. Mux-in-ridge could collapse it to 4-wire I2C. Connector: JST-XH (robust, flex) vs STEMMA QT JST-SH (tiny).
Recommended Action: Decide topology (mux in ridge?) then connector. Keep robust connector on the flex joint.

Flag: Pi is M2.5; chassis is M3 (two fastener systems). Mount method + standoff height undecided — affects bottom plate, service hatch, camera-cable routing.
Recommended Action: Choose M2.5 inserts vs nut-capture; pick standoff height as part of internal stack-up.

Flag: Order adds IMU + air-quality + 2 mics + reed + mux — large expansion past locked camera+ToF+LED. Risk: front-loading all of it stalls v1.
Recommended Action: Sensor count ~10 modalities. Pick minimal v1 (camera+ToF+IMU); rest = plug-in library post-v1 (all STEMMA QT).

Flag: I2S mic uses Pi PCM peripheral; WS2812-style LED band driven via PCM collides with it.
Recommended Action: Drive LED over SPI/PWM (not PCM). Cleanest: hand WS2812 to the KB2040 (PIO) - see F11.

Flag: BNO085 has documented I2C clock-stretching trouble on Raspberry Pi.
Recommended Action: Run BNO085 in UART-RVC or SPI mode, or budget I2C bring-up time.

Flag: Heat-shrink + XT60 solder work need a heat gun; unknown if one is owned.
Recommended Action: Confirm a heat gun is on the shelf; add to acquire list if not.

Flag: Exact XT60H pair count not confirmed from photo.
Recommended Action: Count the bag; update Qty.

Flag: Geekworm standoff kit: 1 or 2 boxes?
Recommended Action: Confirm; update Qty (currently tentative 2).

Flag: Two BNO085s: body+ridge (independent ridge orientation) or 1 + spare?
Recommended Action: Confirm intent; sets whether 2nd IMU is a build item or backup.

Flag: Inline fuse holder needed.
Recommended Action: Closed — 10 in hand.

Flag: Balance charger needed.
Recommended Action: Closed — B6AC V2 in hand.

Flag: Pack puffing if stored at full charge across weekends.
Recommended Action: Mitigated — use charger STORAGE mode (3.8V/cell). Lives on as a habit.

Flag: Connector match between pack and interconnect.
Recommended Action: Closed — pack ships XT60, matches XT60H.

Flag: KB2040 (RP2040) arrived (free). Could be the real-time co-processor for Layer-1 reflex + encoder counting + WS2812/I2S offload - or a spare.
Recommended Action: Decide compute topology: Pi + RP2040 co-pro, or Pi-only. If co-pro, it cleanly resolves F9 and reflex timing.

Flag: I2C address management as device count grows. 2x DS3231 share FIXED 0x68 -> collide on one bus.
Recommended Action: Map every I2C device to bus/mux channel. Colliders (ToF, 2nd RTC) -> separate mux channels; unique-addr -> hub.

Flag: Magnetic sensing intent: 2x DRV5032 Hall + 4x Adafruit reed (375) + ~20 glass reed (E383) all overlap. No defined v1 magnetic job.
Recommended Action: RESOLVED: No magnetic sensor in v1 (core=cam+ToF+IMU per F8). DRV5032 Hall = sensor-of-record for on-chassis magnetics (module-ID, v1.5) - solid-state, vibration-immune. Reeds = library/spare; one good future job = ZERO-POWER dock-presence detect IF a charging dock is added (v1.5+). Reeds parked, not orphaned.

Flag: 5V rail budget: D24V22F5 is 2.5A. Pi 3 active ~0.7-1A; addressable LED band can add ~1A (WS2812 ~60mA/LED at white) + sensors.
Recommended Action: Budget the 5V rail; cap LED count/brightness or move LED band to its own supply if needed.

Flag: Abrupt power-off (kill switch / pack cutoff) can corrupt the Pi's microSD (ext4 on yank).
Recommended Action: Use F3's pack-V sense to trigger a GRACEFUL shutdown before cutoff; and/or run a read-only root filesystem.

Flag: Pi 3 runs warm under vision load; sealed printed body may throttle it.
Recommended Action: Fit kit heatsinks; provide airflow (service hatch as vent / vent slots) in body design.

Flag: Pi 3 model: B or B+? (affects WiFi band + thermals, minor).
Recommended Action: Confirm from board silkscreen.

Flag: Reflex layer (Layer-1, sub-100ms) wants dumb/fast/no-bus sensors - wrong job for I2C gear. 37-in-1 kit supplies them.
Recommended Action: Use kit IR-reflectance (cliff/edge) + shock/tilt (impact/tip) read directly by KB2040 for a deterministic reflex loop, independent of Pi/I2C.

Flag: PIR invalid while moving (field change -> constant false triggers). Also: LWIR (8-14um) won't pass printed PETG/ASA shell, so Fresnel domes must be exposed - protrusions vs no-anthropomorphism spine.
Recommended Action: Use PIR as at-rest presence-attention only: gate output on zero-velocity (encoders/IMU). Design dome openings into shell now. Reframe: stillness-gated 'other present' significance.

Flag: Five PIRs: perimeter array for omnidirectional at-rest coverage, or spares?
Recommended Action: If array: 5 GPIO (consider KB2040, or OR down to 'presence anywhere' if bearing not needed).

Flag: v1.5 camera-articulation servo selection. 10x SG90-class 9g analog (Smraza S51) in hand - ideal for prototyping the pan/tilt mechanism + geometry. Open questions for the FLIGHT build: analog 9g jitter/buzz conflicts with QD's deliberate legible-motion premise; plastic gears back-drive/strip on shock-load.
Related Parts: Smraza S51 9g servo x10; camera ridge; F13 (5V rail)
Recommended Action: Prototype articulation on these. For flight, evaluate digital metal-gear micro servo (smooth/quiet/repeatable holds, gear robustness). Put 2x servos on a dedicated buck/rail w/ decoupling - add stall current to F13 budget. Decide PWM source (Pi sw-PWM jitters -> consider PCA9685). Confirm pan throw: +-120 needs linkage ratio vs ~180deg servo. Keep servo PWM in BODY (ridge connector is sensor-only, per F6).
